concerns.text: Phase 5 (US-3) is entirely skipped with no implementation tasks for FR-007's edge-case handling (logging skipped segments with N<30). The spec mandates this logic for data hygiene even if the dataset is blocked; the task list omits the 'skip and log' mechanism, violating FR-007's requirement to 'skip segments with N < 30 by logging a JSON object'.

Task T022 implements a 'two-proportion z-test' per spec FR-005, but the plan's 'Complexity Tracking' explicitly rejects this test for paired data (Ordered vs. Shuffled) in favor of McNemar's test. This creates a semantic weakening of the statistical validity required by the plan's own methodological constraints, violating the plan's directive to use McNemar's test for paired outcomes.

The tasks list omits any implementation for FR-010 (CI Width Stability and Bias Relative to Block-Bootstrap) which the spec requires for real-world data. While US-3 is blocked, the spec's acceptance criteria for real-world data (US-3) include these metrics as the alternative to coverage probability. The task list provides no path to implement these metrics even as a stub or placeholder, violating the spec's requirement to 'measure CI Width Stability' for real data.
